docs.title: CreatureCapture Documentation
docs.text: CreatureCapture turns mob collection into a progression system for Bukkit-based servers. This documentation covers setup, capture bow behavior, configuration, commands, and storage.

docs.title: Compatibility
docs.text: Get Started Configuration Commands

docs.title: What CreatureCapture Does
docs.text:
• Adds a special Capture Bow that can appear in the enchantment table.
  • Lets players capture supported creatures and receive their spawn eggs.
  • Tracks each player's unique captures as a persistent collection.
  • Provides collection, progress, remaining-creatures, and leaderboard commands.

docs.title: Community
docs.text:
• GitHub repository
  • CurseForge page
  • Bukkit page

docs.title: CreatureCapture - Commands & Permissions
docs.text:
Make sure you have installed the plugin before reading this section.

This page documents the current /cc commands and permission nodes.

docs.title: Commands
docs.text: Command Permission Description /cc get creaturecapture.get Give yourself a Capture Bow. /cc reload creaturecapture.reload Reload config.yml. /cc collection creaturecapture.collection Show your captured creatures and collection progress. /cc stats creaturecapture.stats Show your number of unique captures and completion percentage. /cc remaining creaturecapture.remaining Show the creatures you still need to capture. /cc top creaturecapture.top Show the top players by unique captures.

docs.title: Permission Nodes
docs.text: Permission Description creaturecapture.capture Allows capture attempts with capture arrows. creaturecapture.cc Grants broad access to player /cc subcommands. creaturecapture.get Allows /cc get. creaturecapture.reload Allows /cc reload. creaturecapture.collection Allows /cc collection. creaturecapture.stats Allows /cc stats. creaturecapture.remaining Allows /cc remaining. creaturecapture.top Allows /cc top.

docs.title: Permission behavior
docs.text:
• /cc reload checks only creaturecapture.reload.
  • Player-facing subcommands accept either the specific permission node or the broader creaturecapture.cc node.
  • creaturecapture.capture controls whether a player-fired capture arrow can actually capture a mob.

docs.title: Tab completion
docs.text: Tab completion only suggests subcommands the sender is allowed to use.

docs.title: CreatureCapture - Configuration
docs.text:
Make sure you have installed the plugin before editing the configuration.

Edit plugins/CreatureCapture/config.yml, then run /cc reload.

docs.title: Global settings
docs.text: Key Type Default Description droprate integer 3 Chance in percent for the Capture Bow enchantment offer to appear in the enchantment table. captureBowDurability integer 10 Number of shots available on a Capture Bow. Use -1 for unlimited durability. chanceToCapture number 50 Chance in percent for a valid capture attempt to succeed. spawnersCanBeModifiedByEgg boolean true Allow players to use spawn eggs on spawners. When false, right-clicking a spawner with an egg is blocked.

docs.title: Creature availability
docs.text:
Key Type Default Description Mob_eggs map Generated automatically Per-creature toggle that controls whether a creature can be captured and converted into a spawn egg.

Behavior details:

  • The plugin generates Mob_eggs automatically for all alive and spawnable entity types.
  • Missing entries are added automatically when the plugin detects new entity types.
  • Older configs using top-level entity keys are converted into the Mob_eggs section.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• After changing config values, run /cc reload.
  • Setting a creature to false in Mob_eggs disables capturing that entity.
  • Iron Golem support is special-cased and uses a custom-named spawn egg item.

docs.title: Local development
docs.text:
Requirements:

  • Java 17
  • Gradle wrapper included in the repository

Typical commands:

./gradlew build
./gradlew test
./gradlew printVersion

docs.title: Documentation
docs.text:
This repository uses MkDocs with the shared Stellionix documentation package.

Relevant files:

  • mkdocs.yml
  • requirements.txt
  • docs/
  • .github/workflows/docs.yml

docs.title: CI
docs.text:
• .github/workflows/ci.yml builds the plugin and runs tests.
  • .github/workflows/docs.yml deploys documentation when doc-related files change on main.

docs.title: CreatureCapture - Data Storage
docs.text: CreatureCapture stores collection progress in the plugin data folder.

docs.title: Current storage
docs.text:
• Database file: plugins/CreatureCapture/captures.db
  • Engine: SQLite
  • Key model: one unique entry per player UUID and entity type

This means repeated captures of the same creature do not create duplicate collection entries.

docs.title: Stored information
docs.text:
• Player UUID
  • Latest known player name
  • Entity type name

docs.title: CreatureCapture - Download
docs.text:
You can download CreatureCapture from the following sources:

  • GitHub repository
  • CurseForge
  • Bukkit Dev

For production servers, prefer the latest tagged or published release rather than building from a random branch snapshot.

docs.title: CreatureCapture - How It Works
docs.text: CreatureCapture revolves around a special bow called the Capture Bow.

docs.title: Capture Bow acquisition
docs.text:
• The Capture Bow can appear as a special enchantment-table result on a bow.
  • The chance is controlled by droprate.
  • The special offer only appears on a high-level enchantment roll.

docs.title: Capture flow
docs.text:
1. A player shoots with a Capture Bow.
  2. The fired projectile is tagged as a capture arrow.
  3. If the arrow hits a valid entity, CreatureCapture rolls the configured capture chance.
  4. On success, the target is removed and a spawn egg is dropped.
  5. The creature is stored in the player's collection if it is a new unique capture.

docs.title: Durability behavior
docs.text:
• The Capture Bow uses custom durability shown in lore such as 10/10.
  • Each shot reduces the displayed durability by 1.
  • If captureBowDurability is -1, the lore displays ∞ and the bow never breaks through this system.

docs.title: Supported creatures
docs.text:
• By default, all alive and spawnable entity types are enabled in Mob_eggs.
  • Iron Golems are handled specially through a custom egg item.
  • If a creature does not have a usable spawn egg material, it cannot be captured.

docs.title: Progress tracking
docs.text:
• Captures are stored per player UUID.
  • Duplicate captures do not increase unique collection progress.
  • /cc collection, /cc stats, /cc remaining, and /cc top all read from the same persistent capture store.

docs.title: CreatureCapture - Installation
docs.text: Make sure you have downloaded the plugin first.

docs.title: Install steps
docs.text:
1. Stop your server.
  2. Put the CreatureCapture JAR into your plugins/ folder.
  3. Start the server.
  4. Confirm startup in console logs.

Expected log example:

[CreatureCapture] Enabling CreatureCapture vX.X.X

docs.title: First checks
docs.text:
• Confirm no startup errors are shown.
  • Verify plugins/CreatureCapture/ was generated.
  • Check that config.yml exists inside the plugin folder.
  • Join the server and run /cc collection or /cc stats.

If you encounter issues, check troubleshooting.

docs.title: The Capture Bow never appears in the enchantment table
docs.text:
• Check that droprate is greater than 0.
  • Make sure you are enchanting a plain bow.
  • The special offer only appears on a high-level enchantment roll.

docs.title: Capturing does not work
docs.text:
• Confirm the player has creaturecapture.capture.
  • Check that the target creature is enabled in Mob_eggs.
  • Verify chanceToCapture is not set too low.
  • Some entity types cannot be captured if no matching spawn egg material exists.

docs.title: Commands do nothing
docs.text:
• Check the relevant permission node.
  • For player subcommands, creaturecapture.cc can also grant access.
  • /cc reload is separate and requires creaturecapture.reload.

docs.title: Progress or leaderboard is empty
docs.text:
• Confirm the plugin was able to create captures.db.
  • Check server logs for SQLite initialization errors.
  • If you migrated from an old setup, verify whether captures.yml was renamed to captures.yml.migrated.

docs.title: Spawner interactions are blocked
docs.text: • If spawnersCanBeModifiedByEgg is false, right-clicking spawners with eggs is intentionally blocked.
